sections.paragraph:
Lamed, the twelfth letter of the Hebrew alphabet, as a numeral signifying thirty. Its name (לָמֶד) has the same meaning as מַלְמָד an ox goad, the form of which this letter has on the Phœnician monuments; thus, ነ, ረ.

It is interchanged-

(1) with the other liquid consonants or semi-vowels as-(a) with נ, e.g. לָחַץ and נָחַץ to burn, נִדְנֶה Chald. לְדַן, לִדְנָא a sheath, נִשְׁכָּה and לִשְׁכָּה a chamber, a cell; נָתַן Syr. ܢܬܰܓ to give; in all these examples n appears to be the primitive sound; and on the other hand, ψαλτήιριον, Chald. פְּסַנְתְּרִין, צֶלֶם, Arab. صَنَمُ etc. where n has sprung from l. Compare ἦνθον, βέντιστος, for ἦλθον, βέλτιστος; also the great number of examples from the Arabic, collected by Ev. Scheid, in Diss. Lugd., p. 953.-(b) with ר, commonly in such a manner that ר, as the harder letter, is softened into ל; this is more especially found in later books and dialects, e.g. שַׁרְשְׁרָה a chain, Chald. and Arab. שַׁלְשְׁלָה, سلسلة, and even سنسلة, אַרְמָנֹות palaces, by a softer pronunciation, אַלְמָנֹות Isa 13:22 אֲלוּ and אֲלוּ Chald. lo! קֶרֶב Arab. قَلْبُ midst (compare λείριον, liliun); but on the other hand, elsewhere the exchange is made by l being hardened into r, e.g. אַלְמָנָה, أَرْمَلَةُ, ܙܰܪܡܰܠܬܙܳ a widow, pr.n. סַלְכָה, Arab. صرخت; אלהות Sam. Ecc 3:1-22 Ecc 8:1-17 Dan 4:1-37 Act 7:1-60 godhead; see also the play of words, in לָכִישׁ and רֶכֶשׁ Mic 1:13.-(c) more rarely with מ as גֻּלִגֹּלֶת Abab. جُمْجُمَةُ the skull.

(2) sometimes with ד, as אָזַל Chald. אֲזַל and אֲזַד to go away, depart (which see); compare, besides the examples there cited, Ὀδυσσεύς, Ulysses, odor, and oleo, δαήρ, Lat. levir, etc.

Quadriliterals are sometimes formed from triliterals-(a) by inserting ל after the first radical, as זָעַף, זַלְעַף to glow.-(b) by adding ל at the end, a form which may perhaps, in Phœnicio-Shemitic, as well as in Greek, Latin, and German (see Matthiœ, Greek Gram. § 102; Ramshorn, Lat. Gram. p. 236, 257), have been used in a diminutive sense; see חַרְגַּל (huppeln), כַּרְמֶל, (Gärtchen, קַרְסֹל, (Rnöchel, גִּבְעֹל, (Knötchen, cup of a flower).
